Well just had happen. The Peach Tree just decided was time flower and along comes April snow shower. Looks iffy for peaches again this year. However, given the lack moisture have had this spring five inches April shower truly welcomed event and perhaps will bring May flowers.
The melting snow will keep the temps from going below freezing. They might survive and give you peaches.
Jim, will see was well below freezing last night. This happens most years and peaches seem come only about once every 3-4 years. have tried cover the tree from time time but got lazy this year.
Crossing fingers for the peaches!
Thanks, going take little luck and sunshine. Hope your day going well.
Very pretty like Japanese print, but little sad, too.
Eliza, yes there yin-yangness the late season snow this year. Probably another year without peaches yet sure did need the moisture and all the plants will very happy.
captures natures moment harsh reality beautifully Mike.
Thanks Val. Hop your spring treating you well.

CVEK independent organisation that strives contribute building more just and cohesive society through research, analytical and educational activities. The Centre was founded 2005 and based Bratislava. Their common mission make systematic effort aimed creating environment which every person has every right feel like valuable and respected human being.
Their work rooted defending fundamental human rights principles such respecting dignity every person and promoting diversity society. The activities CVEK focus particularly ethnic, language and religious minorities. Thematically, they place emphasis especially education, housing, social status, interethnic relations and political participation. Their activities may divided into three basic pillars: research, advocacy and education.
They specialise promoting inclusion marginalised population groups, the Roma, migrants other minorities.

This was about yard shot using Pulsar Trail with DLOC mount. I'll post video hunting section.

Also, filed down the rail inside .223 mag. Saw Utube that helps line them better. Thanks for the heads and I'll give RNZ Unimag try.
